challenges.title: Input Types
challenges.description: Create a form with four input fields: text, email, password, and number. Each input must have a corresponding label element using the for attribute. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a form with text, email, password, and number inputs -->
<!-- Each input should have a label -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert 'type="text"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="text"'
assert 'type="email"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="email"'
assert 'type="password"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="password"'
assert 'type="number"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="number"'
assert html.lower().count('<label') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 label elements'
assert html.lower().count('for=') >= 4, 'Each label should use the for attribute'
hints: Each label needs a for attribute matching the input's id attribute

challenges.title: Advanced Inputs
challenges.description: Create a form with four advanced input types: date, range, color, and checkbox. Add placeholder attributes to the date input, a required attribute to the checkbox, and min/max attributes to the range input. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a form with date, range, color, and checkbox inputs -->
<!-- Add placeholder, required, and min/max attributes where appropriate -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert 'type="date"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="date"'
assert 'type="range"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="range"'
assert 'type="color"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="color"'
assert 'type="checkbox"' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an input with type="checkbox"'
assert 'placeholder=' in html.lower(), 'Should use the placeholder attribute'
assert 'required' in html.lower(), 'Should use the required attribute'
assert 'min=' in html.lower(), 'Range input should have a min attribute'
assert 'max=' in html.lower(), 'Range input should have a max attribute'
hints: Add min="0" max="100" to the range input
Add required to the checkbox input
